title: Annotating in OneNote to show working live
what_it_is: OneNote's digital ink tools let you write, draw or annotate directly on a page using a stylus, mouse or touchscreen, visible to anyone viewing or sharing that page.
why_use_it: It gives a clear, legible on-screen record of working that would otherwise be written on paper or a physical whiteboard and then lost. The page stays saved and shareable afterwards, so learners can return to it later.
how_to: Open the relevant page in a Class or Staff Notebook.
Select the Draw tab and choose a pen or highlighter.
Annotate directly on the page while sharing your screen or projecting.
Save, or let it auto-save, so the annotated page remains available afterwards.
